_comment: Stage-2 (precision) of the hybrid dataset-paper classifier. Each candidate abstract is embedded with bge-small (doc-side, no query prefix) and scored: dataset_score = max cosine to a positive prototype minus max cosine to a negative prototype. Papers above DS_THRESHOLD (margin) are kept. Positives describe 'this paper contributes a dataset/benchmark resource'; negatives describe 'this paper proposes a method and merely uses datasets'. Edit these to retune precision; re-run embed_classify.mjs --rescore afterwards.
positive: We introduce a new dataset for training and evaluating models on this task.
We present a new benchmark for evaluating model performance across diverse tasks.
We release a large-scale, human-annotated corpus collected and labeled for this problem.
In this paper we construct and publicly release a dataset of labeled examples.
We propose a new benchmark with thousands of examples spanning multiple domains and languages.
This paper contributes a publicly available dataset with detailed annotations and an evaluation protocol.
We build an evaluation suite and leaderboard to benchmark systems on this capability.
negative: We propose a new neural network architecture that improves accuracy on existing benchmarks.
We use standard datasets to train and evaluate our proposed model.
We prove a theoretical bound on the convergence of our optimization algorithm.
We present a new method that achieves state-of-the-art results on several established datasets.
We survey recent advances and summarize open challenges in the field.
We analyze the behavior of large language models and report empirical findings.
